name: Textura (Oracle) Construction Payment Management API
what_it_does: Textura (Oracle) REST API for construction payment management, lien waiver compliance, and subcontractor payment workflow. Enables AI agents to manage subcontractor payment application and approval workflow for construction payment management, handle lien waiver collection and management for construction compliance, access compliance document management (insurance, licenses, safety certs) for subcontractor qualification, retrieve payment status and disbursement tracking for construction cash flow management, manage retainage tracking and release for construction contract management, handle construction payment draw schedule and submission workflow, access project-level budget and cost tracking for construction project controls, retrieve contractor prequalification and document status for procurement compliance, manage payment disputes and resolution workflow for construction financial management, and integrate Textura payment data with construction ERP, banking, and accounting platforms.
use_cases: Managing subcontractor lien waivers from construction payment agents
Tracking compliance documents from contractor qualification agents
Processing payment draws from construction project agents
Managing retainage from construction accounting agents
not_for: Manufacturing without construction contract payment workflow
Consumer payments without construction subcontractor management
Small projects without enterprise construction payment scale
best_when: A general contractor, developer, or construction manager using Textura (Oracle) wants AI agents to automate subcontractor payment applications, lien waiver compliance, document management, and payment workflow.
avoid_when: LEGAL RISK: Lien waiver automation must account for 50 state-specific lien waiver form requirements — automated lien waiver processing without state law validation creates mechanic's lien rights failures. Payment automation that releases retainage without substantial completion verification creates contract and lien law exposure. Textura was acquired by Oracle (2016, $663M) and integrated into Oracle Construction and Engineering; API is now Oracle CX/Construction platform.

auth.notes: Textura (Oracle) uses OAuth 2.0 (Oracle Identity Cloud Service). Developer portal at developer.oracle.com. Webhooks for payment events. REST API with JSON. Austin, Texas HQ (Oracle). Oracle acquired Textura (2016, $663M). Public (ORCL NYSE). Textura integrated into Oracle Construction and Engineering Cloud. 5,000+ construction project customers using Textura CPM. Strong in GC payment management and lien waiver compliance. Competes with Procore payment management and GCPay for construction payment workflow.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription plus per-project fees
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription; per-project and per-payment transaction fees
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas. Oracle (ORCL NYSE). Textura acquired 2016 ($663M). Oracle Construction and Engineering Cloud. 5,000+ construction project customers. Competes with GCPay and Procore Pay for construction payment management.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per Oracle IDCS client; Oracle Cloud limits apply

known_agent_gotchas: LEGAL RISK: Lien waiver automation must apply correct state conditional/unconditional form; incorrect lien waiver type releases or preserves lien rights incorrectly creating construction law liability

Retainage release automation — automated retainage release requires substantial completion determination; premature release without contract milestone validation creates contract and lien rights issues
Oracle IDCS authentication complexity — Textura OAuth flows through Oracle Identity Cloud Service; IDCS configuration and token management adds complexity beyond standard OAuth flows
Compliance document expiration — automated payment release without current insurance certificates creates general contractor exposure for subcontractor injuries and incidents
Oracle Construction cloud integration — Textura CPM integrates with Oracle Primavera and Oracle ERP; full payment workflow requires Oracle Construction and Engineering suite configuration

50-state lien law variation — construction lien laws vary significantly by state in notice requirements, timing, and waiver forms; automated lien waiver generation requires state-specific legal configuration
agent_readiness.security_notes: Construction payment. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2/Oracle IDCS. US/EU. Construction financial and compliance data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Construction payment. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Payment and lien waiver data.
